title: OMA3 Common Definitions
description: Shared data types and proof structures for OMATrust schemas.

Proof.description: Cryptographic proof submitted to the Relayer to prove the validity of the attestation. Uses a uniform envelope pattern: proofType identifies the proof format, proofObject contains the proof content. The proofPurpose at wrapper level provides quick access, but MUST match the proofPurpose within signed content.

proofType.description: Identifies the proof format and verification method.

proofPurpose.description: Purpose of the proof. Use 'shared-control' for Binding/Linking, 'commercial-tx' for Reviews. MUST match proofPurpose in signed content.

proofObject.description: Proof content. Can be a string (JWS) or object (structured proof data). Structure depends on proofType.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature proving control of the Ethereum address.

chainId.description: EIP-155 chain ID of the signer's chain.

message.description: The EIP-712 typed data message that was signed. Field names are human-readable for wallet display.

signingPurpose.description: Purpose being authorized. Use 'shared-control' for Binding/Linking, 'commercial-tx' for Reviews.

creationTimestamp.description: Unix timestamp when the signature was created. Use 0 if unused.

expirationTimestamp.description: Unix timestamp when the signature expires. Use 0 if unused.

randomValue.description: 32-byte random value for replay protection. Use 0x00...00 if unused.

statement.description: Human-readable safety statement shown by wallets. MUST state this is not a transaction or asset approval.

proofObject.description: RFC 9449 DPoP-style JWS (JSON Web Signature). Claims MUST include: 'oma3_proof_purpose' ('shared-control' or 'commercial-tx'), 'iss' (issuer DID), 'aud' (audience DID), 'iat' (issued at), and 'exp' (expiration).

oneOf.title: Transaction with Encoded Value
oneOf.description: On-chain transaction where the value encodes the proof purpose. No additional signature required as the transaction itself is cryptographically signed.

proofPurpose.description: Purpose of the proof. Must match the encoded value in the transaction.

oneOf.title: x402 Receipt Proof
oneOf.description: Server-signed receipt proving a commercial transaction occurred. Stored as the complete x402 artifact with format discriminator.

proofPurpose.description: x402 receipts are always for commercial transactions.

proofObject.description: Complete x402 signed receipt as returned by the service. The 'format' field discriminates between EIP-712 and JWS.

format.description: Signature format. Determines interpretation of other fields.

payload.description: Receipt payload fields (required for EIP-712, omitted for JWS since payload is embedded in the JWS compact string). Contains: version, network, resourceUrl, payer, issuedAt, and optionally transaction.

signature.description: For EIP-712: hex-encoded ECDSA signature (0x-prefixed, 65 bytes). For JWS: compact serialization string (header.payload.signature).

issuedAt.description: Unix timestamp when receipt was issued.

transaction.description: Optional blockchain transaction hash. Empty string if omitted.

signature.description: JWS compact serialization containing the receipt payload.

oneOf.title: x402 Offer Proof
oneOf.description: Server-signed offer proving a service committed to specific commercial terms prior to payment.

proofPurpose.description: x402 offers are always for commercial transactions.

proofObject.description: Complete x402 signed offer as returned by the service. The 'format' field discriminates between EIP-712 and JWS.

format.description: Signature format. Determines interpretation of other fields.

payload.description: Offer payload fields (required for EIP-712, omitted for JWS). Contains: version, resourceUrl, scheme, network, asset, payTo, amount, and optionally validUntil.

signature.description: For EIP-712: hex-encoded ECDSA signature (0x-prefixed, 65 bytes). For JWS: compact serialization string (header.payload.signature).

validUntil.description: Unix timestamp when the offer expires. 0 if unused.

signature.description: JWS compact serialization containing the offer payload.

oneOf.title: Transaction Interaction Proof
oneOf.description: Reference to an on-chain transaction proving interaction with a service contract. Used to verify actual service usage for reviews and attestations.

proofPurpose.description: Purpose of the proof. Typically 'commercial-tx' for service usage.

txHash.description: Transaction hash proving on-chain interaction with the service. Verifiers should look up the transaction on-chain to validate the interaction.

contractAddress.description: Optional contract address that was interacted with. Helps verify the transaction was with the correct service.

oneOf.title: Evidence Pointer
oneOf.description: URL pointing to external evidence (e.g., a proof posted on a social platform, a document, or other verifiable content). The proof at the URL contains proofPurpose within its signed content.

proofPurpose.description: Evidence pointers are typically used for identity linking.

url.description: Publicly accessible URL containing the full proof (pop-jws or pop-eip712). Verifiers MUST fetch this URL, parse the content to find the embedded proof object, and verify it using the standard pop-jws or pop-eip712 verification process.

PayloadVersion.title: Payload Version
PayloadVersion.description: Semver describing the shape of the payload.

PayloadSpecURI.title: Payload Specification URI
PayloadSpecURI.description: URI to the JSON schema specification for this attestation type.

PayloadSpecDigest.title: Payload Specification Digest
PayloadSpecDigest.description: Integrity digest for payloadSpecURI. Paste the full JSON object.

algo.title: Payload Spec Hash Algorithm
algo.description: Hash algorithm used for payload specification digest.

canon.title: Canonicalization Method
canon.description: Canonicalization method: 'raw' for binary/text files, 'jcs' for JSON (RFC 8785).

hex.title: Payload Spec Hash Value
hex.description: 0x-prefixed hex digest of the payload specification.

Outcome.title: Outcome
Outcome.description: Overall outcome of an assessment or certification. If omitted, outcome MUST be interpreted as 'pass'.

ReportDigest.title: Report Digest
ReportDigest.description: Content digest of a report for integrity verification. Paste the full JSON object.

algo.title: Report Hash Algorithm
algo.description: Hash algorithm used for report digest computation.

canon.title: Canonicalization Method
canon.description: Canonicalization method: 'raw' for binary/text files, 'jcs' for JSON (RFC 8785).

hex.title: Report Hash Value
hex.description: 0x-prefixed hex digest of the canonical report bytes.
